items.title: Tottenham manager latest, Ratcliffe reaction, FA Cup, Thiago’s new deal and more – live
items.summary: Latest news, previews and press conferences Ten things to look out for | Email David here Brentford striker Igor Thiago has signed a new contract, extending his deal until 2031, with the option for an additional 12 months. The Brazilian was signed from Club Brugge in February 2024, but had to overcome a knee injury last season before hitting form this term, scoring 17 Premier League goals so far. “I love the club and the people in the club,” said Thiago. “It’s a true love, a real love. When the fans support me, and I see them singing my name and singing my song, it gives me more power. It’s been a great season for us. Everybody has been on the same page. I hope we can get something special from it.” Continue reading...

items.title: Alles over WK-uitvalsbasis Oranje in Kansas City, die niet in Kansas ligt
items.summary: Donderdag werd bekend dat het Nederlands elftal tijdens de groepsfase van het WK 2026 in de Verenigde Staten, Mexico en Canada verblijft in Kansas City. De uitvalsbasis ligt overigens dan weer niet in de staat Kansas. Alles over de verblijfplaats van Oranje op het WK.

items.title: Slot rueful as Liverpool injury strikes again
items.summary: Arne Slot has admitted the "last thing" Liverpool need this season is another injury after midfielder Wataru Endo was stretchered off against Sunderland.

items.title: Rice tells Arsenal to ignore noise after PL lead cut
items.summary: Declan Rice has urged Arsenal to "block out the outside noise" but insisted his teammates remain calm after missing the opportunity to move six points clear.

items.title: Barcelona taught 'lesson' by Atlético in Copa rout
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his side were taught a lesson by Atlético Madrid as they suffered a 4-0 loss at the Metropolitano on Thursday in the first leg of the Copa del Rey semifinal between the two teams.

items.title: Tuchel 'not sure' if Bellingham fit for England
items.summary: Thomas Tuchel has admitted he's "not sure" if Jude Bellingham will be fit for England's friendlies next month.

items.title: 'Incredible' Álvarez leads Atleti thrashing of Barça
items.summary: Diego Simeone said Julián Álvarez's goal and assist in Atlético Madrid's 4-0 Copa del Rey thrashing of Barcelona will help the forward reset after ending an 11-game scoring drought.

items.title: Harde woorden na vernedering Antwerp: 'Een gemiste kans'
items.summary: Antwerp FC-trainer Joseph Oosting herkende zijn eigen ploeg niet terug in de verloren halve finale van het bekertoernooi tegen Anderlecht ( 0-4 ). De return op eigen veld liep uit op een ongekende afstraffing, die na afloop tot harde woorden leidde.

items.title: De onverwachte geboorte van een topspits
items.summary: Igor Thiago (24) brak door in Bulgarije en scoorde vorig seizoen niet één keer voor Brentford, maar inmiddels is hij de Braziliaan met de meeste doelpunten ooit in een Premier League-seizoen. Vrijdag verlengde hij zijn contract tot medio 2031 . Wie is deze mysterieuze spits die in 2024 voor 37 miljoen euro werd gekocht van Club Brugge?

items.title: Brentford langer door met Premier League-sensatie Thiago
items.summary: Brentford heeft een belangrijke slag geslagen. De nummer zeven van de Premier League heeft het contract van Igor Thiago opengebroken. Hij ligt nu vast tot medio 2031.

items.title: Vier interlands in elf dagen: schema Oranje in Nations League bekend
items.summary: Het schema van het Nederlands elftal in de Nations League is bekend. Oranje start op donderdag 24 september met een thuiswedstrijd tegen Duitsland, dat op maandag 16 november ook de laatste opponent is in Groep 2 van Divisie A.

items.title: Will Skubala's Lincoln win 'biggest game in 65 years'?
items.summary: In the last decade or so, Lincoln have been promoted from non-League, played in an FA Cup quarter-final, been to Wembley a few times and won a first trophy there. Yet Saturday is being branded as the club's biggest game in 65 years.

items.title: Zimbabwe stun Australia at T20 World Cup
items.summary: Zimbabwe beat Australia by 23 runs at the T20 Cricket World Cup on Friday in Colombo.

items.title: Hull's 'complete nobody' out to stun Chelsea on Rosenior return
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at Hull City boss Sergej Jakirovic and how the unheralded appointment has them challenging for promotion to the Premier League.

items.title: 41 and thriving! | LeBron becomes oldest NBA player to record triple-double!
items.summary: Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James recorded 28 points, 12 assists, and 10 rebounds in his team's win over the Dallas Mavericks, becoming the oldest NBA player to score a triple-double.

items.title: ‘He would fill you with confidence’: how Liam Rosenior made his mark at Hull
items.summary: Chelsea manager, back at Hull in the Cup on Friday, made a strong impression on Humberside, as ex-colleagues explain “That’s the best message I’ve received,” Liam Rosenior wrote, accompanied by laughing emojis, in response to one of the many congratulation posts sent after his surprise appointment as the Chelsea head coach. His former Hull teammate James Chester had wickedly told him: “After my time with you at Derby I never thought you’d have been Chelsea manager,” alongside a photograph of Chester in the pub with his fellow former Tigers Robbie Brady and Paul McShane, raising a glass to Rosenior. It was a sign of the close bonds Rosenior built at Hull as a player and head coach. He returns as a Champions League head coach on Friday in the FA Cup, looking to avoid an upset against a team aiming to make it a more regular rivalry. Rosenior spent seven seasons on Humberside, five as a player and two in the dugout, which were crucial for his development. Continue reading...

items.title: Northern Ireland boss O'Neill set to take over at Blackburn
items.summary: Northern Ireland manager Michael O'Neill is set to join Championship club Blackburn, initially in a joint role until the end of the season.

items.title: Ireland captain Stirling out of T20 World Cup
items.summary: Ireland captain Paul Stirling has been ruled out of the rest of the T20 World Cup because of a knee injury.

items.title: To boo, or not to boo? Do players notice and why do fans do it?
items.summary: Booing at football is an accepted part of the game. It's been around since time began, but are fans increasingly booing their own team?

items.title: Will Arsenal's end-of-season form haunt them again?
items.summary: Arsenal are four points clear at the top of the Premier League with 12 games to play - but will recent history repeat itself?
